title: Related Graph Retrieval Follow-up
findings: 发现 3 个新 distinct related-graph 问题：store-qualified personal 边绕过 KT→KP 隐私过滤、fabric-connect/archive 宣称的 review 写边路径实际缺失、related 扩展未应用 layer-flip redirect。
issues_found: [{"title":"Store-qualified personal related IDs bypass the KT→KP topology-leak guard","severity":"high","description":"The graph privacy guard promises to strip team-source related edges that target personal KP entries, but it calls parseKnowledgeId(targetId) directly. Current skills and cross-store recall use store-qualified IDs such as personal:KP-GLD-0001, and parseKnowledgeId only accepts bare KP-/KT- IDs, so decoded becomes null and the edge is allowed through unchanged. A team canonical entry can therefore persist a pointer to a personal knowledge node, violating the §4 privacy iron law and leaking personal graph topology.","location":"packages/server/src/services/knowledge-meta-builder.ts:1107","fix_direction":"Normalize related targets before layer checks: accept alias-qualified/global-ref forms by extracting the trailing local KP-/KT- id, then run the KT→KP guard; add regression tests for related: [personal:KP-...] and bare KP targets, and align fabric-archive/fabric-connect examples with the accepted canonical form.","affected_components":["knowledge-meta-builder related frontmatter parser","fabric-archive related-edge authoring","fabric-connect graph edges","cross-store recall privacy boundary"],"tags":["related-graph-retrieval","privacy","store-qualified-id","frontmatter"]},{"title":"fabric-connect/archive cannot persist related edges through fab_review despite documenting that path","severity":"medium","description":"fabric-connect says to write related edges via the fabric-review modify path, and fabric-archive says session_context related lines survive to approve-time frontmatter authoring, but the actual FabReview modify schema only permits title/summary/layer/maturity/tags/relevance_* and review.ts only rewrites those fields. extractKnowledge also has no related input and renderFreshEntry never emits related. The documented graph-generation workflow therefore has no supported MCP write surface for canonical related frontmatter unless an agent hand-edits files, which the skill forbids.","location":"packages/shared/src/schemas/api-contracts.ts:884","fix_direction":"Add related?: string[] to FabReview modify changes, service ModifyChanges, rewriteFrontmatterMerge/appendPatchLines, parse/list/search output as needed, with validation for existing stable IDs and KT→KP privacy; or update skills to a real supported write path if review should not own graph edits.","affected_components":["FabReviewInputSchema","review modify service","fabric-connect skill","fabric-archive skill","related frontmatter authoring"],"tags":["related-graph-retrieval","fabric-connect","fabric-review","schema-runtime-drift"]},{"title":"Related-edge expansion ignores knowledge_id_redirect mappings after layer flips","severity":"medium","description":"recall rewrites only caller-supplied input.ids through loadIdRedirectMap before intersection, while include_related reads candidate.description.related raw and compares those raw IDs to current candidate IDs. planContext does the same related append before loading/surfacing redirects. If a related target was layer-flipped, review emits knowledge_id_redirect, but a stored related edge to the prior ID no longer expands or nudges even when the new ID is in the active candidate set.","location":"packages/server/src/services/recall.ts:97","fix_direction":"Apply the redirect map to related targets in both recall include_related and planContext include_related before candidate-set lookup, and add tests where A.related points to an old stable ID whose new ID is surfaced after modify-layer.","affected_components":["fab_recall include_related","planContext include_related","id-redirect","fabric-review modify-layer","knowledge graph retrieval"],"tags":["related-graph-retrieval","redirects","include_related","layer-flip"]}]
